title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Bolivia mulls recognizing USDT as payment currency amid dollar shortage
summary: As foreign currency reserves remain under pressure, Bolivia is considering a framework to let the world’s largest stablecoin be used for payments, savings and trade.

title: First Public Fusion Firm Jumps in Wall Street Trading Debut
summary: General Fusion Group Ltd. surged in its first day of trading after merging with a blank-check firm, a sign of investors’ interest in the world’s first publicly listed nuclear fusion company.

title: Trump Restarts Iran Blockade as US Plans 20% Hormuz Charge
summary: President Donald Trump reinstated the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded a 20% reimbursement on all other cargo shipped through the waterway.

title: New Hampshire Follows Bitcoin Reserve With 'Blockchain Basic Laws' Signing
summary: New Hampshire's newly-signed crypto law introduces protections for users, miners, and stakers within its boundaries.

title: Claude's Personality Changes Depending on the Model—And the Language You Speak
summary: According to new Anthropic research, Claude consistently expresses different values across models and languages.

title: Hyperliquid’s HIP-3 markets surge to nearly 50% of perp volume as onchain stock trading grows
summary: HIP-3's share of total Hyperliquid perp volume has climbed from roughly 2% at the start of the year to around 50% now.

title: Gondor v1 to let users borrow against their entire Polymarket portfolio
summary: Expected to launch in September, v1 will enable users to margin their entire Polymarket portfolios to place additional leveraged bets.
